data.title: Low density polyethylene (LDPE) export prices drift lower in the United States
data.description: This week, LDPE export prices down adjusted in the US.An industry source in the U.S. informed a Polymerupdate team member, "The prices of low density polyethylene (LDPE) experienced a decrease over the past week, indicating persistent weakness in demand. Nevertheless, there has been a slight uptick in purchasing interest relative to early April. As the market approaches May, the outlook for export activities remains ambiguous, with continuous pressure for reduced prices affecting overall confidence. Reports regarding inventory levels were varied�some sources indicated that warehouses are close to full capacity, while others observed a slight reduction in stockpiles compared to the more congested conditions of April. FAS Houston LDPE export prices were assessed at USD 985-1005/mt, a slide of USD (-30/mt) from the previous week.Spot upstream ethylene prices on Thursday were assessed at the 19.50-20.00 cents/lb FOB U.S. Gulf levels, a week on week rise of (+1 cent/lb).The US ethylene contract price for April 2025 settled at the 31.50 cents/lb mark. This price represents a decline of 3.75 cents/lb from its March 2025 settlement levels."

data.title: SIM Technology Books Lower Profit in April
data.description:
SIM Technology Group recorded a total revenue of HK$25.8 million in April, down 39% from HK$42 million in March, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The mobile communications company's revenue from the core business fell 36% to HK$22.2 million in April from HK$34.5 million a year prior.

For the four months ended April 30, total revenue rose 32% to HK$140.6 million from HK$106.6 million in the year.

The changes in revenue were attributed to adjustments by customers in the delivery time of their purchase orders.

data.title: Vor Biopharma Cut to Neutral From Buy by HC Wainwright & Co.
Tesla Supplier Laying Off 10,000. Panasonic Stock Rises. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Panasonic reported fiscal fourth-quarter earnings and announced restructuring actions, including laying off 10,000 workers, all designed to improve profitability.

The shares were rising.

The Japanese electronics company on Friday announced fiscal fourth-quarter earnings per share of about 23 cents from sales of $14.2 billion. Wall Street was looking for about 12 cents and $13 billion, respectively.

Looking ahead, the company expects to generate sales of about $54 billion in fiscal 2026, up from about $53.5 billion in 2025. That guidance excludes the auto business, which was sold in December. Wall Street is currently projecting $53.5 billion

Tariffs haven't been factored into the guidance. Panasonic sales in the U.S. total almost $11 billion annually.

All of Panasonic's business, including electric-vehicle batteries, are expected to grow in 2025. Panasonic sells EV batteries to Tesla and others. The forecast partly shows that EV growth should continue around the globe for the coming 12 months.

Operating profit should be about $3.4 billion, or about 6.4% of sales. Wall Street is modeling $3 billion.

Panasonic also laid out plans to improve profit margins to 10% of sales, including a workforce reduction of some 10,000 people. The company has more than 200,000 employees worldwide who make everything from washing machines to motors to EV batteries to TVs and other consumer products.

Panasonic shares were up 2.2% in overseas trading, leaving them up about 6% this and about 27% over the past 12 months. S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average futures were up 0.3% and 0.1%, respectively.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

data.title: Lost Money on Supernus Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (SUPN)? Contact Levi & Korsinsky Regarding an Ongoing Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 9, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Supernus Pharmaceuticals, Inc. ("Supernus Pharmaceuticals, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On February 19, 2025, The company announced that its Phase 2b study of SPN-820 for treatment-resistant depression failed to meet its primary endpoint, showing no significant improvement over placebo. This outcome comes as a surprise to investors, given the company's previous optimism surrounding the drug's potential.

Following this news, shares of Supernus Pharmaceuticals fell over 13% in trading. To obtain additional information, go to:

data.title: Lost Money on Apple Inc. (AAPL)? Contact Levi & Korsinsky Regarding an Ongoing Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 9, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Apple Inc. ("Apple Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

Apple announced its upcoming release of the new iPhone 16 and 16 Plus models on June 10, 2024. The Company heavily marketed these new iPhones as being "built for Apple Intelligence," a suite of generative AI features integrated into iOS 18 and its devices, including a major upgrade to Siri, Apple's voice assistant. Apple touted the new, upgraded AI-driven Siri as "more conversational," capable of increased understanding of user context, and being powered by ChatGPT, a state-of-the-art natural language processing model developed by OpenAI. Apple claimed that the iPhone 16 and 16 Plus were designed from the ground up to support Apple Intelligence, redefining smartphone use in the new AI economy.

Then, Apple announced on March 7, 2025, that the availability of Apple Intelligence would be significantly delayed, and that the Company "needed more time to finish developing the new Siri features" that would enhance Siri's AI capabilities and anticipated "rolling them out in the coming year."

Following this news, the price of Apple common stock fell over 5% on March 10, 2025. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/apple-inc-lawsuit-submission-form-2?prid=147673&wire=1

Kymera Therapeutics Q1 Loss Widens, Collaboration Revenue Rises
data.description:
Kymera Therapeutics reported a Q1 net loss Friday of $0.82 per diluted share, widending from a $0.69 loss a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected a loss of $0.88.

Collaboration revenue for the quarter ended March 31 was $22.1 million, compared with $10.3 million a year earlier.

Kymera shares were up 1.6% in recent premarket activity.

data.title: UK Watchdog's Review Finds 'Most' Smaller Asset Managers Meet Regulatory Standards
data.description:
The UK Financial Conduct Authority's review of smaller asset management companies found that most of them are meeting regulatory expectations, according to a Thursday release.

The FCA's findings focused on high-risk investments, conflicts of interest and the consumer duty. While most of the companies clearly categorized high-risk investments and made "good" progress in implementing the consumer duty, some lacked sufficient processes to ensure high-risk investments were sold to clients only when appropriate, the regulator said.

On the other hand, "many" companies were found to have ineffective arrangements for managing conflicts of interest, particularly in those where senior staff held multiple roles.

The regulator said it will continue to monitor and work with companies that need to make improvements to ensure compliance.

data.title: Earnings Flash (PAA) Plains All American Pipeline LP Reports Q1 Revenue $12.01B, vs. FactSet Est of $14.14B
Stocks Rise Pre-Bell as Investors Parse US-UK Agreement, Await China Trade Talks
data.description:
The benchmark US stock measures were pointing higher before the open Friday amid optimism after Washington struck an agreement with the UK, while investors await an expected meeting between US and Chinese officials over the weekend.

The S&P 500 rose 0.3%, the Nasdaq added 0.4%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average ticked up 0.1% in premarket activity. The indexes closed Thursday's trading session higher, representing the second consecutive day of gains.

US President Donald Trump on Thursday announced a trade agreement with the UK. As part of the deal, the US will raise $6 billion in external revenue from 10% tariffs, $5 billion in "new export opportunities" while creating an aluminum and steel trading zone, and a "secure" pharmaceutical supply chain, Trump said in a social media post.

"Many more (deals) to come," according to Trump.

"While exemptions will nibble away at the effective tariff rate, the baseline 10% is not going anywhere," Oxford Economics said in a Thursday note. "The average US tariff is still set to remain in double digits, which will deliver a big hit to real incomes in the US, causing growth to slow sharply in the second half of the year."

US and Chinese officials are due to meet in Switzerland over the weekend to discuss economic and trade matters. "We're gonna have a good weekend with China," Trump said Thursday, according to The Wall Street Journal.

The two-year US Treasury yield retreated 2.7 basis points to 3.87% in premarket action, while the 10-year rate inclined 0.5 basis points to 4.38%.

With no major economic data scheduled for Friday, traders will focus on commentary from several key central bank officials for clues on future monetary policy and economic outlook, including Federal Reserve Governors Michael Barr, Adriana Kugler, and Christopher Waller. New York Fed President John Williams is also scheduled to speak today.

The Federal Open Market Committee kept interest rates in the range of 4.25% to 4.50% for a third straight meeting on Wednesday and flagged rising uncertainty about the economic outlook.

Shares of Pinterest jumped 14% pre-bell as the image-sharing platform company issued an upbeat second-quarter revenue outlook. Monster Beverage declined 2.2% as the company's first-quarter sales missed estimates. Lyft climbed 8.4% as the ride-hailing company surprisingly swung to a first-quarter profit.

Enbridge , EchoStar and Essent Group report their latest financial results before the bell, among others. Hawaiian Electric Industries posts earnings after the markets close.

West Texas Intermediate crude oil increased 1.9% to $61.07 a barrel before the opening bell. The weekly Baker Hughes domestic oil-and-gas rig count is due at 1 pm ET.

Gold edged 0.7% higher to $3,329 per troy ounce. Bitcoin advanced 2% to $103,180.

data.title: SWK Holdings Announces First Quarter 2025 Earnings Release Date and Conference Call
data.description:
DALLAS, TX / ACCESS Newswire / May 9, 2025 / SWK Holdings Corporation ("SWK" or the "Company"), a life science-focused specialty finance company catering to small- and mid-sized commercial-stage companies, today announced that it will issue its first quarter 2025 earnings results after the market close on Thursday, May 15, 2025. The Company will host a conference call to discuss its corporate and financial results on Friday, May 16, 2025 at 10:00am ET.

The dial-in for the conference call is (888)-506-0062 or (973)-528-0011 for international callers. The participant code is 903493. A live webcast can also be accessed through the following link: https://www.webcaster4.com/Webcast/Page/3029/52355.

A replay of the conference call will be available on the SWK Holdings website shortly following the call.

About SWK Holdings Corporation

SWK Holdings Corporation is a life science focused specialty finance company partnering with small- and mid-sized commercial-stage healthcare companies. SWK provides non-dilutive financing to fuel the development and commercialization of lifesaving and life-enhancing medical technologies and products. SWK's unique financing structures provide flexible financing solutions at an attractive cost of capital to create long-term value for all SWK stakeholders. SWK's solutions include structured debt, traditional royalty monetization, synthetic royalty transactions, and asset purchases typically ranging in size from $5.0 million to $25.0 million. SWK also owns Enteris BioPharma, a clinical development and manufacturing organization providing development services to pharmaceutical partners as well as innovative formulation solutions built around its proprietary oral drug delivery technologies. Additional information on the life science finance market is available on the Company's website at www.swkhold.com.

data.title: Acciona Signs EUR100 Million Total Return Equity Swap Deal
data.description:
Spanish infrastructure and renewable energy conglomerate Acciona entered into a total return equity swap agreement with an unnamed global financial institution.

The total return equity swap for Acciona shares is for a notional amount of 100 million euros, or a maximum of 815,661 shares, according to a Friday release. The swap will mature in 12 months and will be settled in cash.

data.title: Trade Desk Shares Climb After 1Q Revenue Surge
data.description:
By Dean Seal

Shares of Trade Desk jumped after the company reported a strong first quarter from soaring revenue.

The stock was up 14% at 68.55 in premarket trading. Shares had fallen 49% year-to-date when the market closed Thursday.

The platform for buyers of advertising posted a first-quarter profit of $50.7 million, or 10 cents a share, compared with $32 million, or 6 cents a share, in the same quarter a year earlier.

Stripping out one-time items, adjusted earnings were 33 cents a share. Analysts polled by FactSet had been expecting 25 cents a share.

Revenue surged 25% to $616 million, topping analyst forecasts for $575.3 million, according to FactSet.

Trade Desk is guiding for at least $682 million in revenue for the current quarter, just under analyst estimates for $683.7 million.

The company said volatility in the economy has made 2025 an important year for marketers looking to use the open internet to drive business differentiation and growth.

Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.title: TeraWulf 1Q Loss Widens After Bitcoin Halving Cuts Revenue
data.description:
By Dean Seal

TeraWulf said its loss in the first quarter widened as revenue sank due to rising power prices and the halving of Bitcoin last year.

The bitcoin-mining company posted a loss of $61.4 million, compared with a loss of $9.6 million in the same quarter a year earlier.

Per share, the loss was 16 cents. Analysts polled by FactSet had been expecting a loss of 7 cents a share.

Revenue fell 19% to $34.4 million, below analyst projections for $41.3 million, according to FactSet.

The decline was caused by headwinds from the April 2024 halving of Bitcoin, a change occurring every four years that halves the number of bitcoins released on average every 10 minutes.

Increased network difficulty and rising power prices also cut into the top line, partially offset by a higher average bitcoin price and expanded mining capacity.

TeraWulf also said Friday that its board has refreshed an authorization for a $200 million stock-repurchase program.

Write to Dean Seal at dean.seal@wsj.com

data.title: Sylvamo Delivers Results In Line With Outlook, Well Positioned With Strong Balance Sheet
data.description:
Sylvamo, the global paper company, has released its first quarter 2025 earnings report, revealing a mixed performance compared to the previous quarter. The company, which operates mills in Europe, Latin America, and North America, has faced several operational challenges but remains optimistic about its future outlook.

Financial Highlights

For the first quarter of 2025, Sylvamo reported a net income of $27 million, translating to $0.65 per diluted share, a significant drop from the $81 million ($1.94 per diluted share) reported in the fourth quarter of 2024. Adjusted operating earnings also saw a decline, coming in at $28 million ($0.68 per diluted share) compared to $82 million ($1.96 per diluted share) in the previous quarter. Adjusted EBITDA was $90 million, representing an 11% margin, down from $157 million and a 16% margin in the fourth quarter of 2024. Cash provided by operating activities was $23 million, a sharp decrease from $164 million in the previous quarter, and free cash flow was negative at $(25) million, compared to $100 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.

Business and Operational Highlights

The company faced several challenges in the first quarter, including a $10 million unfavorable price and mix impact due to paper price decreases in Europe and Brazilian export regions. Volume decreased by $30 million, attributed to seasonally weak demand in Latin America and operational challenges in North America. Additionally, operations and other costs increased by $12 million, driven by unfavorable foreign currency exchange rates and operational issues in North America. Planned maintenance outage expenses rose by $9 million, and input and transportation costs increased by $6 million due to higher energy prices and extreme cold weather across the United States.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Sylvamo returned nearly $40 million in cash to shareholders in the first quarter, distributing $18 million via dividends and repurchasing $20 million in shares. The company has $62 million remaining on its $150 million share repurchase authorization from September 2023. Additionally, the board of directors declared a second-quarter dividend of $0.45 per share, which was paid on April 29.

In terms of leadership changes, Jean-Michel Ribiéras, the current Chairman and CEO, announced his retirement at the end of the year. John Sims, who served as the company's first senior vice president and CFO, will take over as CEO on January 1, 2026. Don Devlin has been appointed as the new senior vice president and CFO, effective May 1.

Management's Perspective

Jean-Michel Ribiéras highlighted the completion of a heavy planned maintenance outage schedule in Europe and North America during the first quarter. He noted that the second quarter would be the heaviest outage quarter of the year, with over 80% of the total annual planned maintenance outage costs expected to be spent by mid-year. Ribiéras also emphasized the company's strong balance sheet, with a 1.1x leverage ratio and no major maturities until 2027, allowing Sylvamo to navigate the uncertain economic environment.

Future Outlook

For the second quarter, Sylvamo expects adjusted EBITDA to be in the range of $75 million to $95 million. The company anticipates improvements in price and mix, operations and other costs, and input and transportation costs. However, planned maintenance outage expenses are expected to increase by $36 million. Management expects quarterly earnings to significantly improve in the second half of the year, benefiting from lower planned maintenance outage expenses, improved commercial results, and better operations.

SEC Filing: Sylvamo Corp [ SLVM ] - 8-K - May. 09, 2025
